$comment: Scoring weights for AI-readiness audit v1.3. Weights are normalized to sum to 1.0. Each dimension weight reflects its relative importance to AI agent consumption of the design system. Adjust per client context without modifying the audit schema or prompt.

phase_readiness_thresholds.$comment: Score thresholds for phase_readiness in the audit summary. A system with any blocker-severity finding is 'not_ready' regardless of score.

token_implementation.$comment: Highest weight. Without token implementation, an AI agent cannot resolve any design values programmatically. This is the foundation everything else depends on.

alias_chain_integrity.$comment: Highest weight, tied with token implementation. Broken alias chains produce silent failures in code generation -- the agent resolves to the wrong value or a dead reference. Undetectable without explicit checking.

token_architecture_depth.$comment: High weight. Three-layer architecture (primitive/semantic/component) determines whether an agent can reason about intent vs raw value. A flat token set forces the agent to guess context.

component_to_token_binding.$comment: High weight. Unbound properties mean the agent must infer which token applies to which component property -- a frequent source of hallucination in code generation.

primitive_naming.$comment: Medium weight. Machine-parseable naming is required for automated token mapping, but an agent can partially compensate for naming issues if bindings and aliases are intact.

naming_convention_consistency.$comment: Medium weight. Inconsistent naming across tokens, components, and styles degrades pattern matching but does not block agent use outright.

web_readiness_gap.$comment: Medium weight. Missing ARIA roles and accessibility metadata create gaps between Figma representation and production code, but the agent can flag these rather than fail silently.

component_description_coverage.$comment: Lower weight. Descriptions improve agent reasoning about when and how to use a component, but absence does not block code generation -- it reduces quality of contextual decisions.

governance.$comment: Lower weight. Governance rules improve long-term maintainability and drift detection, but their absence does not directly impair a single audit or code generation run.

documentation_quality.$comment: Lowest weight at this stage. Documentation quality improves agent reasoning but is the least machine-critical dimension. A system with perfect tokens and bindings but no docs is more AI-ready than the reverse.
